content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here, you need to find the exact value of RRR.**

You are given an integer NNN.

An array AAA is called good if it satisfies all of the following conditions:


1≤Ai≤N1 \le A_i \le N1≤Ai​≤N for all 1≤i≤∣A∣1 \le i \le |A|1≤i≤∣A∣
Ai≤Ai+1A_i \le A_{i + 1}Ai​≤Ai+1​ for all 1≤i<∣A∣1 \le i < |A|1≤i<∣A∣
A1+A2+…+A∣A∣=NA_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_{|A|} = NA1​+A2​+…+A∣A∣​=N
A1×A2×…×A∣A∣=NA_1 \times A_2 \times \ldots \times A_{|A|} = NA1​×A2​×…×A∣A∣​=N
In other words, the array should consist of non-decreasing positive integers and its product and sum both must be NNN.

Here, ∣A∣|A|∣A∣ denotes the length of AAA.

Let RRR denote the number of good arrays. Your task is to find RRR. It can be proven that the answer does not exceed 101810^{18}1018.

For example, for N=6N = 6N=6, R=2R = 2R=2 because there are 222 good arrays, [6][6][6] and [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value RRR.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one array A=[1]A = [1]A=[1].
Test case 444: There are 222 arrays: A=[4]A = [4]A=[4] and A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2].
